title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things: Building a Business When There Are No Easy Answers
description: A brutally honest, anecdote-driven account of building and running a startup through its darkest moments — from the Silicon Valley investor and former CEO who lived through near-bankruptcy, firing friends, and the crushing psychological burden of leadership.
keyConcepts: The right way to lay people off
The quiet thing everyone knows but no one talks about
Fight for the soul of the company
notableQuotes: The Struggle is when you wonder why you started the company in the first place.
Take care of the people, the products, and the profits — in that order.
When it comes to bad news, there is no such thing as too much communication.
Peacetime CEO does what a peacetime CEO does. Wartime CEO does what a wartime CEO does. They are completely different jobs.
The most important thing to remember is this: nobody cares.
If you are going to eat shit, don't nibble. Bite.
There are no silver bullets. Only lead bullets.
The quiet thing that everyone knows but no one talks about will destroy your company.
CEOs should tell it like it is.
